title: Untrusted Assets, Save Parsing, Paths, Privacy, and Local Security · Language Clinic: Safe Serialization
summary: A mod folder escapes its root, decompression consumes memory, pickle loads scores, save files are unlimited, crash reports include home paths, and telemetry starts without consent. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the safe serialization increment in bounded local-data and privacy policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory…
objectives: Use bounded validated data formats, reject unsafe object loading, write atomically, and recover without hiding corruption.
Constrain asset, settings, replay, and score paths to approved roots and reject traversal, symlink, type, and size violations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Asteroids team handles case 4535 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound asset and save paths, sizes, parsing, integrity purpose, privacy, telemetry consent, and untrusted-content behavior; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-security-save-assets-3
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-security-save-assets-3.
# Case 5798: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def worked_asteroids_security_save_assets_3(untrusted_paths: int = 6, rejected_paths: int = 6, save_size: int = 4096, save_limit: int = 65536, remote_content: bool = False, telemetry_opt_in: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "playerworked3",
        "seed5798",
        "input3dekrow",
        "frameworked35798",
        "state5798worked3",
        "playtest3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if untrusted_paths < 0 or rejected_paths < 0:
        return False, rejected_paths, "security-domain"
    if rejected_paths != untrusted_paths:
        return False, rejected_paths, "path-admission"
    if save_size < 0 or save_size > save_limit:
        return False, save_size, "save-budget"
    if remote_content or not telemetry_opt_in:
        return False, save_size, "external-data"
    assert save_size <= save_limit
    return True, rejected_paths, "bounded-local-data"

steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-safe-serialization-1p9zl3" that uses case 5070 to bound asset and save paths, sizes, parsing, integrity purpose, privacy, telemetry consent, and untrusted-content behavior. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-safe-serialization-1p9zl3
# Competency: asteroids-safe-serialization.
# Case 5070: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision.
# Changed case: lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely.
def evidence_asteroids_safe_serialization_1p9zl3(untrusted_paths: int = 6, rejected_paths: int = 6, save_size: int = 4096, save_limit: int = 65536, remote_content: bool = False, telemetry_opt_in: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1p9zl3 = "1p9zl3-3lz9p1-5070"
    assert evidence_variant_1p9zl3
    evidence_axes_1p9zl3 = (
        "player1p9zl3",
        "seed5070",
        "input3lz9p1",
        "frame1p9zl35070",
        "state50701p9zl3",
        "playtest3lz9p15070",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1p9zl3)) == 6
    if untrusted_paths < 0 or rejected_paths < 0:
        return False, rejected_paths, "security-domain"
    if rejected_paths != untrusted_paths:
        return False, rejected_paths, "path-admission"
    if save_size < 0 or save_size > save_limit:
        return False, save_size, "save-budget"
    if remote_content or not telemetry_opt_in:
        return False, save_size, "external-data"
    assert save_size <= save_limit
    return True, rejected_paths, "bounded-local-data"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Safe Serialization evidence · Theory Safe Serialization, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Safe Serialization precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Safe Serialization evidence · Theory Safe Serialization, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 1488 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound asset and save paths, sizes, parsing, integrity purpose, privacy, telemetry consent, and untrusted-content behavior; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Use bounded validated data formats, reject unsafe object loading, write atomically, and recover without hiding corruption.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Safe Serialization evidence · Theory Safe Serialization, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Local pickle data is safe because only the player can edit it.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Safe Serialization model · Theory Safe Serialization
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Safe Serialization: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 6345 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound asset and save paths, sizes, parsing, integrity purpose, privacy, telemetry consent, and untrusted-content behavior; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Use bounded validated data formats, reject unsafe object loading, write atomically, and recover without hiding corruption.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Safe Serialization model · Theory Safe Serialization.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Safe Serialization model · Theory Safe Serialization, keep Safe Serialization connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use bounded validated data formats, reject unsafe object loading, write atomically, and recover without hiding corruption.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: asteroids-safe-serialization-1p9zl3.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: asteroids-safe-serialization-1p9zl3(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_asteroids_safe_serialization_1p9zl3\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?rejected_paths != untrusted_paths)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?save_size > save_limit)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?remote_content)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not telemetry_opt_in)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
